creator: mike@jellydonut.org
text:
Problem still appears to exist, although the behavior effects are not as bad as before. -tr flag does not seem to work (no transparency to background image is visible), but the -sh flag does appear to enable shading initially. When a -sh window is moved around, the background image is static and the shading appears to be cumulative every time the window is moved (shades darker and darker each time it is moved), eventually the shading fades completely to black. Window artifacts still seem to appear as well. I'm also continuing to see the "BadMatch" errors in the terminal that I use to start aterm as originally mentioned in this PR.

I've attached a screenshot of the artifact behavior to better describe what I'm seeing. The light blue "foreground" text is from my most recent ls command, and the dark blue "background" text behind it is from the ls command preceding that, so new text seems to overlay on top of text that should have been cleared out of the window. If I scroll up or down in the window, the foreground text scrolls but the background text stays static in the window. Also, when scrolling, I continue to see new "BadMatch" errors...

aterm has encountered the following problem interacting with X Windows :
      Request: 62,    Error: 8(BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes))
      in resource: 0x1C00060
aterm has encountered the following problem interacting with X Windows :
      Request: 62,    Error: 8(BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes))
      in resource: 0x1C00060
aterm has encountered the following problem interacting with X Windows :
      Request: 62,    Error: 8(BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes))
      in resource: 0x1C00060
aterm has encountered the following problem interacting with X Windows :
      Request: 62,    Error: 8(BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes))
      in resource: 0x1C00009



Thanks!
Michael Proto
